source_intake_record.raw_question_text: <image_1> An 'arrow-pushing' diagram is a common type of chemical image used to illustrate electron flow in mechanistic steps. The transition-state structure undergoes changes after the electron has been relocated or reacted. Which of the following options shows the structure after the change? Note: Disregard the arrows in the options. The selected option should represent the immediate product of the next step, not the final product of the reaction.
extraction_notes: answer field is option letter only; mapped to corresponding option content
images are referenced as placeholder tokens rather than explicit file paths or URLs

image_dependency: question_text explicitly asks about an 'arrow-pushing' diagram and which option shows the structure after the change
all choices and the answer are image placeholders (<image_2> to <image_5>, answer <image_4>)
the task depends on visual chemical structure interpretation rather than text alone
multi_step: the prompt requires inferring the immediate product after electron relocation in a mechanistic step
the note says to disregard arrows in the options and select the immediate next-step product, not the final product
the solver must compare multiple visual options (4 choices) against the inferred structural change
this is more than direct lookup, but still a single mechanistic-step multiple-choice question
verifiability: choices are discrete (A/B/C/D) and answer_text identifies a single option image (<image_4>)
image manifest shows the referenced image exists and has usable readability metadata

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present with no registry issues, so the core semantics are intact
verifiability is high because the problem is multiple-choice with a single identified correct option
the sample is strongly image-dependent, but the required visual asset exists and appears usable
specialized chemistry notation and image-placeholder answer add handling complexity, but they do not indicate missing core support
